# launchdarkly_release_pipeline (Resource)

Provides a LaunchDarkly release pipeline resource.

~> **Beta:** This resource wraps a beta LaunchDarkly API. Beta resources may change or be removed in future versions of the provider, and the underlying API may change in backwards-incompatible ways.

This resource allows you to create and manage release pipelines within a LaunchDarkly project. A release pipeline is an ordered set of `phases`, each phase being a logical grouping of one or more `audiences` (LaunchDarkly environments) that flags progress through as they are rolled out. To learn more, read [Release pipelines](https://docs.launchdarkly.com/home/releases/release-pipelines).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "launchdarkly_release_pipeline" "checkout_rollout" {
  project_key = launchdarkly_project.example.key
  key         = "checkout-rollout"
  name        = "Checkout rollout"
  description = "Roll out checkout changes from internal testing to general availability"

  phases = [
    {
      name = "Internal testing"
      audiences = [
        {
          environment_key = "staging"
          name            = "QA team"
        },
      ]
    },
    {
      name = "General availability"
      audiences = [
        {
          environment_key = "production"
          name            = "All customers"
          configuration = {
            release_strategy = "manual"
            require_approval = true
            notify_team_keys = ["release-managers"]
          }
        },
      ]
    },
  ]

  tags = ["checkout", "terraform-managed"]

  depends_on = [launchdarkly_project.example]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) The unique key that references the release pipeline. A change in this field will force the destruction of the existing resource and the creation of a new one.
- `name` (String) A human-friendly name for the release pipeline.
- `phases` (Attributes List) An ordered list of the release pipeline's phases. Each phase is a logical grouping of one or more audiences that share attributes for rolling out changes. Must contain at least one phase. The order is significant — flags progress through the phases in order. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--phases))
- `project_key` (String) The release pipeline's project key. A change in this field will force the destruction of the existing resource and the creation of a new one.

### Optional

- `description` (String) A description of the release pipeline's purpose.
- `is_project_default` (Boolean) Whether this release pipeline is the default pipeline for its project. Can only be set at creation time; the update API does not support changing it. A change in this field will force the destruction of the existing resource and the creation of a new one.
- `tags` (Set of String) Tags associated with the release pipeline.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) The release pipeline's creation date represented as a UNIX-style timestamp, in milliseconds since the epoch.
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource in the format `project_key/key`.
- `version` (Number) The version of the release pipeline.

<a id="nestedatt--phases"></a>
### Nested Schema for `phases`

Required:

- `audiences` (Attributes List) An ordered list of the audiences for this phase. Each audience corresponds to a LaunchDarkly environment. Must contain at least one audience. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--phases--audiences))
- `name` (String) The release phase name.

<a id="nestedatt--phases--audiences"></a>
### Nested Schema for `phases.audiences`

Required:

- `environment_key` (String) The key of the LaunchDarkly environment this audience targets.
- `name` (String) The audience name.

Optional:

- `configuration` (Attributes) Release strategy and approval configuration for this audience. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--phases--audiences--configuration))
- `segment_keys` (Set of String) Segment keys targeted by this audience. Omit the attribute when the audience targets no segments; an explicit empty set is not supported.

<a id="nestedatt--phases--audiences--configuration"></a>
### Nested Schema for `phases.audiences.configuration`

Required:

- `release_strategy` (String) The release strategy for this audience.

Optional:

- `notify_member_ids` (Set of String) Member IDs notified to review the approval request. Only meaningful when `require_approval` is `true`.
- `notify_team_keys` (Set of String) Team keys whose members are notified to review the approval request. Only meaningful when `require_approval` is `true`.
- `require_approval` (Boolean) Whether this audience requires approval before changes are rolled out. Defaults to `false`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# A release pipeline can be imported using the project key and release pipeline
# key joined by a slash: project_key/pipeline_key.
terraform import launchdarkly_release_pipeline.checkout_rollout example-project/checkout-rollout
```
